text.title: generator-critic-bounded-loop Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: `while not critic(output).is_perfect():` — no cap, no plateau detection.
antipattern.reason: Perfect-is-enemy-of-good drives unbounded spend; one critic regression can drain the budget.

section.p: Symptom: loop runs 10+ iterations in production. Root cause: missing cap. Fix: enforce hard cap of 3 with absolute maximum 5; add plateau detector.

antipattern.description: Generator and critic share same model + same temperature.
antipattern.reason: The critic rubber-stamps the generator's mistakes because they share the prior. Loop adds cost without quality.

section.p: Symptom: critic score climbs across iterations but eval-set quality flat. Root cause: shared prior. Fix: swap critic model, swap temperature, or rewrite the rubric to force a different reasoning path.

antipattern.description: Critic returns only a numeric score; loop decides `should_continue` from a fixed threshold.
antipattern.reason: The critic has more context than the threshold rule; threshold-based logic leaks into caller code and is hard to tune.

section.p: Symptom: caller code has score thresholds duplicated across multiple loop sites. Root cause: critic schema missing `should_continue`. Fix: force the critic to emit `should_continue: bool` explicitly; remove threshold logic from caller code.

antipattern.description: Tool-calling agent uses both a critic AND a tool result as truth sources.
antipattern.reason: Two truths means the loop has no clear stop condition; critic may veto a correct answer because it disagrees with the tool, or vice versa.

section.p: Symptom: loop oscillates between approve and reject. Root cause: dual truth. Fix: pick one. If the tool is authoritative, drop the critic; if the critic must judge, the critic should incorporate tool output into its reasoning, not compete with it.
